title: dryopteris layardii
intro: Dryopteris layardii, also known as the Lord Howe Island fern, is a species of fern native to the Lord Howe Island in Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Dryopteris layardii include its potential to aid in reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Dryopteris layardii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal preparation methods.
